text:
On a quiet Wednesday afternoon, 20-year-old Charlie found himself at a crossroads. His bed was cluttered with empty snack wrappers and textbooks, his phone was off because he didn’t want to be disturbed, and his apartment was a battlefield of half-complete homework and half-empty popcorn bags. As the sun dipped below the horizon, casting a warm, orange glow on the cityscape, Charlie sat down at his desk and closed his eyes.

A deep, rhythmic breathing brought him home for a moment. He opened his eyes and stared at the ceiling, a little less cloudy. The ticking clock on his nightstand reminded him it was time to move—time to finally buckle down and face the day ahead. Focusing on his goal, he decided his first step was setting a clear intention for what he wanted to achieve.

Charlie grabbed a pen and his notepad, writing down what he wanted to accomplish. He wrote it slowly at first—something as simple as finish one chapter of his novel
